feed.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin

entry.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
entry.summary:
The document titled "Shielded CSV" available at [eprint.iacr.org/2025/068](https://eprint.iacr.org/2025/068) presents a proposal for enhancing the trustless interoperability between blockchain systems, specifically focusing on Bitcoin (BTC). It suggests that implementing a soft fork compatible with BIP-441 could enable the construction of a trustless bridge. This bridge would facilitate secure and private transactions without compromising the decentralized nature inherent to cryptocurrencies.
The proposal underscores the importance of upgrading current systems to support more advanced security features while maintaining user privacy through shielded transactions. The integration of BIP-441 is critical as it would provide the necessary framework to ensure that these transactions remain verifiable yet confidential across different blockchain networks. This development is aimed at bolstering the robustness and efficiency of BTC transactions, potentially broadening its use case in the digital economy.
